title: dodonaea attenuata var. subintegra
intro: Dodonaea attenuata var. subintegra, also known as the 'Hooked Leaf' or ' Small-leaved Hop Bush', is a shrub native to Australia. The leaves and bark of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Dodonaea attenuata var. subintegra include relief from fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as potential protection against infection. In herbal preparations, the leaves can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, while the bark can be used to create extracts and ointments.
